frontierComparison.interpretation: The deterministic 29-bucket rank boundary accounts for every post-post-post-post-successor bucket but exposes the whole 29-bucket q-avoiding cover as the next finite token. This is a ranked handoff, not global convergence; q163/q167 singleton descent remains blocked.

assembledPieces.claim: The 26-bucket post-post-post-successor q-avoiding cover classified every source q-avoiding class with no survivors and emitted 29 post-post-post-post-successor obstruction-prime buckets.

The deterministic rank-boundary packet accounts for all 29 buckets and records the row-uniform two-root law at q in {163,167,173,179,181,191,193,197,199,211,223,227,229,233,239,241,251,257,263,269,271,277,281,283,293,307,311,313,331}.
The next finite token is the whole 29-bucket q-avoiding cover with 1,256,125,158,212,428,260,162,381,710,030,390,124,682,911,240,345 q-avoiding classes.
No q163, q167, or other singleton q-child descent is allowed until a later packet consumes a smaller finite token or closes the whole 29-bucket cover.

supportedClaim.claim: The 29-bucket deterministic rank boundary is assembled into a single whole-boundary q-avoiding cover handoff.
supportedClaim.proofBoundary: This assembly selects the next finite token. It does not close the root children, the q-avoiding cover, the p479 branches, q97, p443-unavailable, the p4217 complement, collision-free matching, or Problem 848.

compressionCandidate.statement: A repeated two-root q-avoiding cover lemma may compress this ladder, but this packet only assembles the emitted rank boundary into the next whole-boundary q-cover token.
compressionCandidate.missingForUse: A theorem proving uniform closure or a finite rank decrease for all future two-root successor buckets.

remainingPuzzleBoundary.openBoundary: The 29-bucket q-avoiding cover, its root children, p479-available and p479-unavailable branches, q97, p443-unavailable, the wider p4217 complement, collision-free matching, and Problem 848 remain open.

oneNextAction.action: Run the whole 29-bucket post-post-post-post-successor q-avoiding batch cover, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or exact survivor/rank boundary.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: If the whole-boundary cover does not close, emit a deterministic survivor/rank boundary grouped by q and previous support; do not open q163, q167, or any singleton q-child first.
oneNextAction.whyCheaperThanSingleSelector: The exact rank packet already names the whole finite q-cover token, so a singleton q selector would fragment the measured boundary instead of consuming it.
oneNextAction.completionRule: Either all 29-bucket q-avoiding classes are covered, or an exact survivor/rank boundary for the whole token is emitted.

nextTheoremMove: Run the whole 29-bucket post-post-post-post-successor q-avoiding batch cover or emit a structural/survivor boundary; singleton q descent remains blocked.
proofBoundary: This convergence assembly consumes the deterministic 29-bucket rank-boundary handoff and selects the whole q-avoiding cover. It does not close the 34,245,622,822,721,856,501,206,493,630,956,387,547,552,030 root children, the 1,256,125,158,212,428,260,162,381,710,030,390,124,682,911,240,345 q-avoiding classes, the p479 branches, q97, p443-unavailable, the p4217 complement, collision-free matching, or Problem 848.
